title: The circular flow model and GDP
description: Practice what you have learned about the circular flow model and how gross domestic product (GDP) is calculated in this exercises.
topic_path: Economic indicators and the business cycle
The circular flow model and GDP

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best definition of a country's gross domestic product (GDP)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The total amount of exports a country sells to the rest of the world in a specific time period
The total value of consumer products purchased by all of the households in a country in a specific time period
The total value of the goods and services produced by the citizens of a country in a specific time period, both within the country and beyond its borders
The total value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period
The total value of the goods and services purchased by a country's government in a specific time period

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why does the measurement of a country's GDP not include the value of all intermediate goods used in the production of a finished good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Because the value of intermediate goods would be over-counted, and GDP would appear much larger than it actually is
Because the value of intermediate goods would be under-counted, and GDP would appear much smaller than it actually is
Because intermediate goods aren't always produced within a country's borders, so we cannot count them towards the country's GDP
Because the value of intermediate goods would be over-counted, and GDP would appear much smaller than it actually is
Because the value of finished goods does not include the value of intermediate goods

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the role of households and firms in the circular flow model of the economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Households supply the factors of production to firms in exchange for money; firms supply goods and services to households in exchange for money
Households provide firms with factors of production in exchange for goods and services
Firms provide households with factors of production in exchange for money; households provide firms with goods and services in exchange for money
Firms provide households with the factors of production in exchange for goods and services
Firms and households exchange money with one another in the pursuit of higher incomes and profits

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the circular flow model, how is the GDP of a nation determined?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By adding the total income of households to the total expenditures by households and the total revenues of firms
By adding the total income of households to the total revenues of firms
By adding the total expenditures of households to the total income of households
By adding the total revenues of firms to the total expenditures of households
By determining either total household income, total household expenditures, or the total revenues of all the firms in the nation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What measure is the value of the final output produced within the geographic borders of a country?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

In Fitlandia a $\$100 \text{ million}$ skyscraper is constructed over two years. In year $1$, $\$60\text{ million}$ is spent on its construction, and in year two $\$40\text{ million}$ is spent. 

**How will the construction of these skyscrapers impact Fitlandia's gross domestic product (GDP) in year $1$ and year $2$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increases by $\$100\text{ million}$ in year $1$; unaffected in year $2$ GDP
Unaffected in year $1$; increases by $\$100\text{ million}$ in year $2$
Increases by $\$60\text{ million}$ in year $1$;  increases by $\$40\text{ million}$ in year $2$
Increases by $\$40\text{ million}$ in year $1$; increases by $\$60\text{ million}$ in year $2$
Unaffected in year $1$; unaffected in year $2$

assessment_items.question_content:
Spending by households is counted in the consumption category of gross domestic product, but there is one kind of household spending that is included in the investment category instead.

**Which type of household spending is recorded as investment towards a country's gross domestic product?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**A change in which of the following will have no effect on the country's GDP?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Angelica owns a factory. She must replace \$10,000 worth of equipment in the factory every year because it wears out.

**What is the term used to describe when capital wears out?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following describes the amount of new capital that is added to an economy?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following expenditures would be counted as investment towards the United States' gross domestic product (GDP)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A European airplane manufacturer builds a new assembly plant outside of Atlanta, Georgia
Ford Motor Company builds a new factory in Mexico
A recent high school graduate invests in a college education
The US government spends $\$1\text{ billion}$ on a new interstate highway
A Wall Street investment bank purchases a large number of shares in a young Silicon Valley tech company

assessment_items.question_content:
The Frank-Heggood Corporation began 2018 with \$50 million in capital. In 2018, the Frank Heggood Company spent \$400 million on inventory, \$200 million on new capital, and experienced 10% depreciation of its capital. 

**Based on this information, what is the Frank Heggood Company’s contribution to gross domestic product (GDP) in 2018, and in what category is that contribution counted?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: \$195 million is counted in investment, and \$400 million is counted in consumption.

assessment_items.question_content:
Burgin, Burgin, and Burgin, LLC began 2016 with \$100 million in capital. In 2016, the company spent \$30 million on inventory, \$40 million on new capital, and experienced 10% depreciation of the capital it had at the beginning of 2016. 

**Based on this information, what is the this company’s contribution to gross domestic product (GDP) in 2016, and in what category is that contribution counted?


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: \$30 million is counted in investment, and \$30 million is counted in consumption.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows macroeconomic data for the nation of Hamsterville during 2018.

 
Category | Amount of spending, in millions
-|-
Gross private investment | $\$200$ 
All consumption spending produced domestically or internationally | $\$400$
Consumption spending on goods produced internationally | $\$75$
Government spending on goods and services | $\$100$
Goods produced in Hamsterville and sold in other countries | $\$25$
Capital depreciation | $\$60$

**What is the nominal gross domestic product (GDP) in Hamsterville in 2018?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To calculate nominal GDP using the expenditures approach, apply the following equation:

$\text{Nominal GDP} = \text{Consumption} + \text{Investment} + \text{Government spending} + \text{Exports} - \text{Imports}$

The investment category in the equation used to calculate nominal GDP is all private investment done in the economy—in other words, gross private investment. So the $\$60$ of capital depreciation isn’t included in this equation because deducting that amount would mean that we are counting net private investment, not gross private investment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would cause the value of a nation's GDP to increase in a given year?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the sale of used cars
An increase in sale of illegal goods in the economy
More people choose to stay at home to raise their children rather than using paid childcare
Households spend more on domestic air travel

assessment_items.question_content:
**When using the expenditures approach, which category of gross domestic product (GDP) changes when domestic firms anticipate that a recession will occur soon?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
